zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Babington IV於1333年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾盧曉拉福時，他的父親John de Babington III29歲，母親Alice de Bois24歲。他於1353年與Benidicta de La Warde在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾坎布利其結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1409年在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾享年76歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾易亞斯特 布利其福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Babington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name for someone from Babington in Somerset or Great or Little Bavington in Northumberland, which is recorded as Babington until the early 18th century. Both are named with the Old English personal name Babba (see Babb ) + the connective particle -ing- ‘associated with, named after’ + tūn ‘settlement’. The name may also be derived from Bebington in Cheshire (see 2 below) or Bevington in Warwickshire (see Bevington , which has sometimes been altered in spelling by association with Babington).

English (Cheshire and Lancashire): variant of Bebbington, a habitational name from Bebington in Cheshire, from the Old English personal name Bebba + the connective particle -ing- ‘associated with, named after’ + tūn ‘settlement’. The alteration to Bavington and Bevington shows a common development of -b- to -v- in placenames and surnames.

variants.name: Babb
meanings: English (Devon, Somerset, and Cornwall): from the Middle English personal name Babb(e), usually no doubt a pet form of the female personal name Barbara (see Barbara ), but possibly in some cases a survival of the Old English personal name Babba, which is found in several placenames, including Babbacombe in Devon and Babington in Somerset.
English (Devon, Somerset, and Cornwall): from Middle English babe ‘infant, young child’, applied as a nickname.
Americanized form of German   Bobb   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Bevington
meanings: (southwest Midlands): habitational name from either of two places, in Warwickshire and Gloucestershire, named Bevington, from the Old English personal name Bēofa + Old English -ing- implying association + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Babington IV was born in 1333, in Rushcliffe, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, John de Babington III, was 29 and his mother, Alice de Bois, was 24. He married Benidicta de La Warde in 1353, in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1409, in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76, and was buried in East Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Benidicta de La Warde en 1353, à Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1409, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à East Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Babington IVが1333年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアルシュクリフで生まれた時，父John de Babington IIIは29歳，母Alice de Boisは24歳であった。1353年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアカンブリッジでBenidicta de La Wardeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1409年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで76歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアエアスト ブリドグフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Babington IV(이)가 1333년 영국 노틴그함실르 러스h클리프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Babington III(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Bois(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1353년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에서 Benidicta de La Warde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1409년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 이스트 브리지퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Babington IV родился в 1333 г. в местности рушклифф, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Babington III было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Bois, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Benidicta de La Warde в 1353 г. в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1409 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности эаст бриджфорд, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.
